survtrans 1.0.0
  • Reference
    • The Generation of Simulated Survival Data

Simulated Group Survival Data: One-source Transfer Learning

Source: R/sim1.R
sim1.Rd

A dataset containing 2 groups of survival data, where the baseline hazards are homogeneous and the effects are heterogeneous. The first group, also called the source group, contains 900 individuals, while the second group, also called the target group, contains 100 individuals. The survival time of group \(i\) is generated according to the following models: $$ \lambda^{(i)}(t)=\lambda_{0,i}(t)\exp\left(x^\top\beta^{(i)}\right), $$ where \(\lambda_{0,i}(t)\) represents the baseline hazard of group i, \(\beta^{(i)}\) represents the effects of group i, and \(x\) represents the covariates. The covariates \(x=(x_{1},\ldots,x_{20})\) are generated from a multivariate normal distribution with mean 0 and covariance matrix \(\Sigma=I_{20}\). The baseline hazard function $$ \lambda_{0,i}(t)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll} t^{2}, & i=1, \\ t, & i=2. \end{array}\right. $$ while the effects $$ \beta^{(i)}=\left\{\begin{array}{ll} (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5,0,\ldots,0), & i=1, \\ (-0.5,-0.5,0.5,0.5,0,\ldots,0), & i=2. \end{array}\right. $$ The maximum censoring time is fixed at 2, and the censoring rate is approximately 20%.

Format

A data frame with 1000 rows and 24 variables:

id

Individual identifier, 1-1000.

group

Group indicator, 1=Source, 2=Target.

time

Survival time.

status

Status indicator, 0=censored, 1=event.

X1

covariate 1.

X2

covariate 2.

X3

covariate 3.

X4

covariate 4.

X5

covariate 5.

X6

covariate 6.

X7

covariate 7.

X8

covariate 8.

X9

covariate 9.

X10

covariate 10.

X11

covariate 11.

X12

covariate 12.

X13

covariate 13.

X14

covariate 14.

X15

covariate 15.

X16

covariate 16.

X17

covariate 17.

X18

covariate 18.

X19

covariate 19.

X20

covariate 20.

Source

../articles/simulate-data.html
